items.title: Iraola will make Liverpool fans excited again - Murphy
items.summary: Andoni Iraola is set to be announced as the Liverpool head coach this week - but what is the feeling around the city to his impending arrival?

items.title: Kyle Walker reveals what goes on in England camp during a tournament as Thomas Tuchel’s men kick-off World Cup training
items.summary: THOMAS TUCHEL's biggest challenge at the World Cup is what goes on away from training - not on the field. items.title: Nelson: Don't be surprised if Billam-Smith hits canvas in Rozicki 'ego' battle
items.summary: This may sound a bit crude, but when Chris Billam-Smith and Ryan Rozicki meet in Bournemouth on Saturday, you'll have two cavemen who want to slug it out.

items.title: Eight players Arsenal could sell this summer including Leandro Trossard and Kepa Arrizabalaga with tiny release clause
items.summary: ARSENAL may have ended their 22-year wait for a Premier League title this season, but the Gunners are still hungry for more. items.title: England stars will play in ‘cursed’ 79,000-capacity World Cup 2026 stadium when they face Panama at £1.7billion MetLife
items.summary: THE METLIFE STADIUM will join some of the most iconic football stadiums on the planet by staging the biggest match of all. Wembley, the Azteca, the Maracana and the Bernabeu have all hosted the World Cup final. And on July 19 2026, the MetLife will join the exclusive list when the winning captain -

items.title: Newcastle latest: Magpies join Brighton in race to sign winger
Why do managers struggle after stepping up to 'big-six' clubs?
items.summary: Will Liverpool-bound Andoni Iraola buck the trend of Premier League managers struggling after moving to a so-called 'big-six' club?

items.title: Scheffler looks to make history with third-straight Memorial title
items.summary: Scottie Scheffler is hoping to match Tiger Woods in securing a third-successive Memorial Tournament title this week as the world No 1 looks to join the list of golfing greats to three-peat on the PGA Tour.

items.title: Sabalenka: I just 'want to quit tennis' after French Open collapse
items.summary: A downbeat Aryna Sabalenka says she feels like she "wants to quit tennis" following her shock French Open quarter-final defeat to Diana Shnaider on Wednesday.

items.title: Experience or potential? The key questions Celtic must answer in managerial search
items.summary: Celtic have made some - albeit slow - progress towards filling their managerial vacancy this week. But it's not completely straightforward.

items.title: Ronaldinho rolls back the years alongside F1 drivers at charity match!
items.summary: Ronaldinho rolled back the years as he joined Pierre Gasly, Carlos Sainz and Isack Hadjar at the Racing Stars Football Cup ahead of the Monaco Grand Prix.

items.title: Millwall publish ‘Pride playbook’ to help clubs form stronger links with LGBTQ+ teams
items.summary: Lions seek to help extend the reach of inclusive clubs 78-page document is the first of its kind in football Millwall have published a first-of-its-kind “Pride playbook” to help football clubs form stronger partnerships with LGBTQ+ teams across the country. items.title: World Cup hosts warned over homophobic chant that went viral at last three tournaments as Fifa issue ruling
items.summary: WORLD CUP hosts Mexico have been warned their fans could face punishment over a controversial chant that has echoed around stadiums for more than a decade.
